summary: Parallel review: FAIL (3 critical, 17 warning, 10 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 bin/lib/gate.mjs:95 — `verdict` is exit-code-only but `findings.critical` uses parsed JUnit count; inconsistency can produce invalid handshake state (PASS + critical > 0). Adjust verdict after parsing.
🟡 bin/lib/gate.mjs:206 — `isJUnitXml()` detection is inlined; refactor into dispatcher when remaining parsers are added
🟡 bin/lib/parsers.mjs:33 — `<error>` elements (JUnit runtime exceptions) silently ignored; Maven/Gradle emit these for real failures
🟡 bin/lib/parsers.mjs:1 — File named `parsers.mjs` vs SPEC's `validator-parsers.mjs`; naming divergence
🟡 test/.test-workspace — Non-deterministic timestamp noise committed
🟡 STATE.json:4 — Feature status "completed" but 0/17 tasks completed; misleading
🟡 SPEC.md:30 — File naming (`parsers.mjs` vs spec's `validator-parsers.mjs`) and API surface (1 export vs spec's 5) deviate from spec
🟡 bin/lib/gate.mjs:95 — Verdict/findings inconsistency: exit-code-based verdict can conflict with parsed findings count
🟡 bin/lib/gate.mjs:106 — Auto-detection hardcoded to JUnit only; no dispatcher pattern for future formats
🟡 progress.md:81 — Outcome review claims "no capability delivered" — inaccurate, JUnit parsing works

🔴 bin/lib/gate.mjs:95 — `verdict` is set from exit code only, but `findings.critical` uses parsed JUnit count; when exit=0 and JUnit XML has `<failure>` elements, the gate writes `verdict: "PASS"` with `findings.critical > 0`, which `validateHandshake` rejects as invalid. This is the exact scenario the feature exists to catch, and it produces an invalid handshake. Fix: add `const finalVerdict = effectiveCritical > 0 ? "FAIL" : verdict;` after line 109 and use it everywhere

🟡 bin/lib/gate.mjs:104 — No integration test for the gate+JUnit parsing path; SPEC calls for `test/gate-validator-integration.test.mjs` but it was never created; the verdict bug above lives in this untested code path
🟡 bin/lib/parsers.mjs:33 — `<error>` elements (runtime exceptions) silently ignored; Maven Surefire/Gradle emit these for real failures
🟡 bin/lib/parsers.mjs:43 — CDATA sections pass through raw in failure body messages
🟡 test/.test-workspace/features/backlog-gate-test/STATE.json — Non-deterministic timestamp diffs committed

🔴 **bin/lib/gate.mjs:95** — Verdict derived solely from exit code, never overridden by parsed JUnit findings. Exit code 0 + JUnit `<failure>` elements = PASS verdict with critical findings > 0. Violates SPEC ("FAIL verdict even when exit code is 0") and handshake validation rules. The feature's central security property is not implemented.
🔴 **bin/lib/gate.mjs:146** — Same root cause: handshake `status` and `task.status` in STATE.json are also not overridden, so the task gets marked "passed"/"completed" despite critical findings.
🟡 **bin/lib/parsers.mjs:73** — Dynamic regex in `extractAttr` without escaping `name` parameter (currently safe with hardcoded strings, but fragile).
🟡 **bin/lib/gate.mjs:106** — Loose `isJUnitXml` detection can false-positive on non-XML output containing `<testsuite` literal text.
🟡 **bin/lib/gate.mjs:113** — `taskId` used in path construction without traversal sanitization.
